what: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: R-native implementation STORM (Synthesis Topic Outlines Retrieval Multi-perspective Question Asking) Co-STORM Stanford’s STORM project. package reproduces core workflow primitives: - Multi-perspective research automatically generated expert personas - Evidence tracking citations source attribution - Two-step outline refinement lead section generation - Query decomposition semantic fact retrieval - Parallel research across perspectives (optional) - Interactive multi-agent moderation mind map (Co-STORM) - LLM-driven discourse management dynamic expert roster (Co-STORM) - Automated evaluation simulated users Built R AI ecosystem: - ellmer — LLM orchestration: tool calling, structured output, streaming - ragnar — RAG: chunking, embedding, semantic retrieval - dsprrr — structured extraction/generation modules (optional) - shinychat — interactive chat UI - vitals — evaluation tasks

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: ellmer reads provider credentials environment variables. OpenAI, example:

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: default, tempest uses native provider web search available (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). leverages provider’s built-web search capabilities better results. use alternative search providers: Provider-specific API keys alternative search: - Wikipedia: API key required (fallback native available) - .com: set YDC_API_KEY - Bing: set BING_SEARCH_API_KEY - Serper: set SERPER_API_KEY - Brave: set BRAVE_API_KEY - DuckDuckGo: API key required - Tavily: set TAVILY_API_KEY - SearXNG: set SEARXNG_API_URL; optionally set SEARXNG_API_KEY - Google Custom Search: set GOOGLE_SEARCH_API_KEY GOOGLE_CSE_ID - Azure AI Search: set AZURE_AI_SEARCH_API_KEY, AZURE_AI_SEARCH_ENDPOINT, AZURE_AI_SEARCH_INDEX_NAME

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Use remove_duplicate = TRUE ask polishing step collapse repeated content preserving unique cited claims. can also use single model roles:
code: library(tempest)  cfg <- tempest_config(   # search_provider = "native" is the default (uses OpenAI/Anthropic/Google native search)   models = list(     coordinator = "openai/gpt-4o-mini",     expert = "openai/gpt-4o-mini",     writer = "openai/gpt-4o",     judge = "openai/gpt-4o-mini",     mindmap = "openai/gpt-4o-mini"   ) )  res <- tempest_run("Life cycle assessment of lithium-ion batteries", config = cfg, verbose = TRUE)  cat(res$report_md) cfg <- tempest_config(models = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514")

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Run perspective research parallel using mirai:
code: res <- tempest_run(   "Quantum computing applications",   config = cfg,   parallel_research = TRUE,  # requires mirai   parallel_writing = TRUE,   # also requires mirai   verbose = TRUE )

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Save intermediate STORM artifacts disk resume interrupted runs: run directory includes JSON artifacts perspectives, personas, sources, facts, outlines, references, plus Markdown drafts polished article.
code: res <- tempest_run(   "Quantum computing applications",   config = cfg,   output_dir = "storm-runs" )  # Later: loads completed stages from storm-runs/quantum-computing-applications # and continues from the first incomplete stage. res <- tempest_run(   "Quantum computing applications",   config = cfg,   output_dir = "storm-runs",   resume = TRUE )

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: tempest_run() executes five steps: perspectives, research, outline, write, polish. Key features: Persistent staged runs – pass output_dir save run artifacts completed stage; pass resume = TRUE load saved artifacts skip completed stages. ToC-enriched perspective discovery – seed URLs fetched table--contents headings extracted give coordinator richer context generating expert perspectives. Query decomposition – expert research question decomposed 2-3 targeted search queries retrieval. Semantic fact retrieval – ragnar configured, section writing uses semantic similarity select relevant facts (falls back keyword matching otherwise). Two-step outline – draft outline generated LLM’s parametric knowledge, refined research findings. Lead section – Wikipedia-style lead (2-3 paragraphs) generated prepended article. Parallel section writing – pass parallel_writing = TRUE write report sections concurrently mirai, extract facts assemble article deterministic outline order. dsprrr modules – dsprrr installed, structured extraction steps (query decomposition, fact extraction, outline drafting, section writing) use dsprrr modules reliable structured output. Falls back chat$chat_structured() otherwise.

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Compile STORM modules labeled examples, reuse run:
code: query_train <- data.frame(   question = c("What are battery recycling bottlenecks?"),   topic = c("Life cycle assessment of lithium-ion batteries") ) query_train$queries <- I(list(c(   "lithium battery recycling bottlenecks",   "EV battery recycling capacity constraints" )))  optimized_modules <- tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules(   trainsets = list(query_decomposition = query_train),   config = cfg,   save_dir = "storm-modules" )  res <- tempest_run(   "Life cycle assessment of lithium-ion batteries",   config = cfg,   dsprrr_modules = optimized_modules )

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Enable semantic search fetched sources providing embedding function: embed_fn provided, tempest automatically: - Chunks fetched web content using ragnar::markdown_chunk() - Stores chunks metadata (source_id, url, title, perspective) - Registers semantic retrieve tool chat agents can also provide pre-built ragnar store:
code: library(tempest)  cfg <- tempest_config(   embed_fn = ragnar::embed_openai(),  # or embed_ollama(), custom function   search_provider = "wikipedia" )  res <- tempest_run("Quantum computing applications", config = cfg) store <- tempest_create_ragnar_store(   embed_fn = ragnar::embed_openai(),   cache_dir = "~/.tempest_cache" ) cfg <- tempest_config(ragnar_store = store)

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Use custom LLM providers (e.g., internal APIs) chat_fn parameter:
code: cfg <- tempest_config(   chat_fn = function(role, model, system_prompt, echo) {     my_company_chat(       model = model,       system_prompt = system_prompt,       echo = echo     )   } )

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Register additional tools (e.g., btw) chat agents:
code: cfg <- tempest_config(   tools = btw::btw_tools()   # or: tools = list(my_custom_tool1, my_custom_tool2) )

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Co-STORM provides interactive multi-expert research experience automatically generated expert personas.

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
code: library(tempest)  # Create a session - personas are generated automatically session <- tempest_session("AI safety and alignment", n_experts = 3)  # See who's on the panel session$get_persona_names() #> [1] "Dr. Sarah Chen" "Prof. Marcus Webb" "Dr. Aisha Patel"  # Optional: Run warmup phase (experts research their initial questions) session$warmup(verbose = TRUE) #> ℹ Warmup: Dr. Sarah Chen (3 questions) #>   Q: What are the current technical approaches to AI alignment? #> ℹ Warmup: Prof. Marcus Webb (3 questions) #>   Q: What governance frameworks exist for AI safety? #> ℹ Warmup complete: 18 facts, 12 sources  # Interactive Q&A - moderator delegates to experts via tools result <- session$step("What are the main risks from advanced AI systems?") cat(result$answer)  # Generate report (styles: "technical" or "executive") report <- session$report(style = "technical", include_references = TRUE) cat(report)

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: moderator access expert tools (ask_dr_sarah_chen, ask_prof_marcus_webb, etc.) delegate research questions specialized experts. expert: chat session conversation continuity Can use web_search fetch_url find cite sources Extracts facts automatically response Returns session IDs follow-questions pattern provides clean separation concerns leverages ellmer’s native tool calling.

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: optional warmup phase expert research initial questions interactive Q&begins. primes knowledge base foundational research:
code: # Skip warmup - jump straight into Q&A session <- tempest_session("Quantum computing") session$step("What is quantum supremacy?")  # With warmup - experts research first session <- tempest_session("Quantum computing") session$warmup()  # Each expert answers 2-4 initial questions session$step("What is quantum supremacy?")  # Benefits from prior research

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Enable LLM-driven turn management autonomous multi-agent conversations: discourse manager decides turn’s action: expert speaks, whether add retire experts, whether probe deeper questions, end round.
code: cfg <- tempest_config(enable_discourse_manager = TRUE) session <- tempest_session("CRISPR gene editing", config = cfg) session$warmup()  # Autonomous mode: the discourse manager decides who speaks next result <- session$step(auto = TRUE)

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Experts can added retired session: roster respects max_active_experts (default 5). Retired experts’ tools return notice expert longer available.
code: session$add_expert("quantum error correction") session$retire_expert("Dr. Sarah Chen") session$get_active_personas()

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: node_expansion_trigger_count set, oversized mind map nodes automatically split subtopics:
code: cfg <- tempest_config(node_expansion_trigger_count = 8) session <- tempest_session("AI safety", config = cfg)

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
code: # Reorganize the mind map before generating the report report <- session$report(style = "technical", reorganize = TRUE)

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: app provides: Chat tab: Multi-expert conversation auto-generated personas, shinychat chat_server() streaming, cancellation, greeting support Mind Map tab: Real-time knowledge graph visualization Sources tab: Table retrieved sources metadata Facts tab: Extracted facts citations confidence Report tab: Rendered markdown report footnotes Features: - Configurable number experts optional warmup phase - Report style selection (technical executive) - Dark mode toggle app currently depends shinychat development branch introduces chat_server(), pinned DESCRIPTION.

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
code: library(tempest) library(vitals) library(ellmer)  judge <- ellmer::chat("openai/gpt-4o-mini") tsk <- tempest_task(dataset = "qa", scorer_chat = judge) tsk$get_samples()

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Run automated Co-STORM sessions simulated curious researcher:
code: # Standalone SimulatedUser session <- tempest_session("AI safety") sim <- SimulatedUser$new("AI safety", max_turns = 5) sim$run_session(session, warmup = TRUE, verbose = TRUE) report <- session$report()  # As a vitals task tsk <- tempest_costorm_task(dataset = "qa", max_turns = 5, scorer_chat = judge)

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: package designed starting point reference implementation. use ragnar domain-specific content (internal docs, databases) add guardrails (policy, bias checks) systematic evaluations customize prompts inst/prompts/

title: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R
text: Papers: STORM: Shao, Y., et al. (2024). Assisting Writing Wikipedia-like Articles Scratch Large Language Models. NAACL 2024. Co-STORM: Jiang, Y., et al. (2024). Unknown Unknowns: Engaged Human Learning Participation Language Model Agent Conversations. EMNLP 2024. DSPy: Khattab, O., et al. (2024). DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls Self-Improving Pipelines. ICLR 2024. Code inspired tempest: stanford-oval/storm — reference STORM Co-STORM implementation package ports R. stanfordnlp/dspy — DSPy framework dsprrr brings R tempest’s optimizable modules. Stanford STORM project — live demo project overview.

title: TempestConfig — TempestConfig
text: Holds configuration STORM / Co-STORM sessions: LLM models, prompts, retrieval settings, cache paths.

title: TempestConfig — TempestConfig
text: models Named list model identifiers role. params Additional parameters passed chat creation. chat_fn Custom chat factory function (optional). tools Additional tools register chats (optional). embed_fn Embedding function RAG (optional). ragnar_store ragnar store semantic retrieval (optional). search_provider Search provider name. cache_dir Path cache directory. max_search_results Maximum number search results return. max_search_queries_per_turn Maximum decomposed queries per research turn. retrieve_top_k Maximum facts/chunks retrieved section. max_sources Maximum number sources keep. user_agent User agent string HTTP requests. node_expansion_trigger_count Number notes/sources per node triggers expansion (NULL = disabled). enable_discourse_manager Whether enable LLM-driven discourse management Co-STORM. max_active_experts Maximum number active expert agents Co-STORM. enable_unseen_surfacing Whether surface undiscussed sources Co-STORM.

title: TempestConfig — TempestConfig
code: TempestConfig$new(   models = NULL,   params = NULL,   chat_fn = NULL,   tools = NULL,   embed_fn = NULL,   ragnar_store = NULL,   search_provider = "native",   cache_dir = NULL,   max_search_results = 8,   max_search_queries_per_turn = 3,   retrieve_top_k = 25,   max_sources = 24,   user_agent = "tempest (R; +https://github.com/JamesHWade/tempest)",   node_expansion_trigger_count = NULL,   enable_discourse_manager = FALSE,   max_active_experts = 5L,   enable_unseen_surfacing = FALSE )

title: TempestConfig — TempestConfig
text: models Named list model identifiers role, single string use roles. params Additional parameters passed chat creation. chat_fn Custom chat factory function. accept role, model, system_prompt, echo arguments return ellmer-compatible Chat object. Use custom providers like chat_company(). tools Additional tools register chat. Can list ellmer tools, function returns tools (e.g., btw::btw_tools). embed_fn Embedding function RAG. accept character vector return matrix embeddings. Use ragnar::embed_openai(), ragnar::embed_ollama(), custom function. provided, ragnar store automatically created. ragnar_store pre-built ragnar store. NULL embed_fn provided, store created automatically tempest metadata schema. search_provider Search provider: "native" (use provider's built-web search available), "wikipedia", "", "bing", "serper", "brave", "duckduckgo", "tavily", "searxng", "google", "azure_ai_search". Default "native" uses OpenAI, Anthropic, Google's native web search capabilities available, falling back "wikipedia". cache_dir Path cache directory. max_search_results Maximum search results return. max_search_queries_per_turn Maximum decomposed queries per research turn. retrieve_top_k Maximum facts/chunks retrieved section. max_sources Maximum sources keep. user_agent User agent string HTTP requests. node_expansion_trigger_count Number notes/sources per node triggers expansion (NULL = disabled). enable_discourse_manager Whether enable LLM-driven discourse management Co-STORM. max_active_experts Maximum number active expert agents Co-STORM. enable_unseen_surfacing Whether surface undiscussed sources Co-STORM.

what: tempest: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R — tempest-package
title: tempest: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R — tempest-package
text: Implements R-native clone STORM Co-STORM research--writing workflows using 'ellmer' LLM orchestration 'shinychat' interactive Shiny UI. Includes web retrieval tools, evidence tracking citations, mind-map style topic planning, evaluation helpers built 'vitals'.

title: tempest: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R — tempest-package
text: tempest ports Stanford's STORM Co-STORM research workflows R, optimizable modules built DSPy (via dsprrr). STORM: Shao et al. (2024), Assisting Writing Wikipedia-like Articles Scratch Large Language Models, NAACL 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.14207 Co-STORM: Jiang et al. (2024), Unknown Unknowns: Engaged Human Learning Participation Language Model Agent Conversations, EMNLP 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.15232 DSPy: Khattab et al. (2024), DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls Self-Improving Pipelines, ICLR 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.03714 Upstream implementations inspired package: stanford-oval/storm, stanfordnlp/dspy, dsprrr.

title: tempest: STORM and Co-STORM Research and Writing Agents for R — tempest-package
text: Maintainer: James Wade github@jameshwade.com Authors: James Wade github@jameshwade.com contributors: OpenAI [copyright holder]

Optimize STORM dsprrr modules — tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules
text: Compiles selected STORM dsprrr modules user-provided training data. explicit optimization step: run labeled examples, pass returned module list tempest_run() via dsprrr_modules.

title: Optimize STORM dsprrr modules — tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules
code: tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules(   trainsets,   modules = NULL,   config = tempest_config(),   teleprompter = NULL,   valsets = NULL,   .llm = NULL,   save_dir = NULL,   k = 4L,   seed = 123L,   strict = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Optimize STORM dsprrr modules — tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules
text: trainsets Named list training data frames. Names must match module names tempest_make_dsprrr_modules(), "query_decomposition", "fact_extraction", "section_writing", "lead_section". modules Optional named list modules optimize. Defaults fresh modules tempest_make_dsprrr_modules(config). config TempestConfig object used modules NULL. teleprompter Optional dsprrr teleprompter, named list teleprompters, factory function. Defaults dsprrr::LabeledFewShot(). valsets Optional named list validation data frames. .llm Optional ellmer chat object passed dsprrr compilation. save_dir Optional directory .rds path optimized module list. k Number examples default LabeledFewShot teleprompter. seed Random seed default teleprompter. strict TRUE, abort first compile failure. FALSE, warn keep unoptimized module step. verbose TRUE, print optimization progress.

title: Optimize STORM dsprrr modules — tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ trainset <- data.frame(   question = "What are battery recycling bottlenecks?",   topic = "lithium batteries" ) trainset$queries <- list(c("battery recycling", "EV battery capacity")) modules <- tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules(   trainsets = list(query_decomposition = trainset),   config = tempest_config() ) } # }

title: Run the STORM pipeline — tempest_run
text: scripted workflow : discovers perspectives research questions, runs multi-perspective research loop (search/fetch + expert synthesis), creates outline, writes cited report Markdown.

title: Run the STORM pipeline — tempest_run
code: tempest_run(   topic,   config = tempest_config(),   retriever = NULL,   n_experts = 3,   research_strategy = c("key_questions", "conversation"),   max_rounds = 3,   max_questions_per_perspective = 3,   parallel_research = FALSE,   parallel_writing = FALSE,   dsprrr_modules = NULL,   steps = c("perspectives", "research", "outline", "write", "polish"),   output_dir = NULL,   resume = FALSE,   run_id = NULL,   remove_duplicate = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the STORM pipeline — tempest_run
text: topic Research topic question. config TempestConfig. retriever Optional TempestRetriever. NULL, created config. n_experts Number expert personas use (default 3). research_strategy Either "key_questions" (default, faster) "conversation" (thorough slower). Key questions uses predefined questions; conversation dynamically generates follow-questions. max_rounds Maximum rounds per perspective "conversation" strategy (default 3). max_questions_per_perspective Maximum questions per perspective "key_questions" strategy (default 3). parallel_research TRUE, run research perspectives parallel using mirai package. Requires mirai installed. Default FALSE. parallel_writing TRUE, write report sections parallel using mirai package. Failed parallel sections retried sequentially. dsprrr_modules Optional named list dsprrr modules, typically tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules(). NULL, fresh modules created dsprrr installed. steps Character vector controlling steps run. Defaults . output_dir Optional directory persisted STORM run artifacts. supplied, artifacts written topic-specific subdirectory. resume TRUE output_dir contains previous run, load saved artifacts skip stages recorded complete. run_id Optional run directory name. Defaults slug topic. remove_duplicate TRUE, ask polish step remove duplicate highly repetitive content preserving unique cited claims. verbose TRUE, prints progress messages.

title: Run the STORM pipeline — tempest_run
text: list title, outline, draft_md, report_md, store, output_dir.

title: Run the STORM pipeline — tempest_run
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cfg <- tempest_config() result <- tempest_run("History of jazz", config = cfg) cat(result$report_md) } # }

title: Run STORM asynchronously (Shiny-friendly) — tempest_run_async
text: thin wrapper around tempest_run() returns promise. useful calling STORM Shiny app without blocking session.

title: Run STORM asynchronously (Shiny-friendly) — tempest_run_async
text: ... Arguments passed tempest_run(). See tempest_run() details available parameters including topic, config, retriever, n_experts, research_strategy, max_rounds, steps, verbose.

title: Run STORM asynchronously (Shiny-friendly) — tempest_run_async
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ tempest_run_async("History of jazz", config = tempest_config()) |>   promises::then(function(result) cat(result$report_md)) } # }

title: tempest 0.1.0
text: tempest_config() gains max_search_queries_per_turn retrieve_top_k controls mirror upstream STORM runner’s query section-retrieval limits. tempest_config(search_provider = ) now accepts upstream-style retrievers .com, Bing, DuckDuckGo, SearXNG, Google Custom Search, Azure AI Search addition existing native, Wikipedia, Serper, Brave, Tavily providers. tempest_run() now executes STORM structured steps dsprrr modules dsprrr installed, ellmer fallbacks module creation runtime failures. tempest_optimize_dsprrr_modules(), tempest_save_dsprrr_modules(), tempest_load_dsprrr_modules() support explicit dsprrr compilation reuse optimized STORM module sets. tempest_run() gains output_dir, resume, run_id arguments persistent staged runs JSON Markdown artifacts. tempest_run() gains parallel_writing upstream-style concurrent section writing mirai. tempest_run() gains remove_duplicate upstream-style duplicate removal polish step. tempest_run(parallel_research = ) parallel_writing now start stop mirai daemons require, fall back sequential execution workers unavailable, retry failed perspective section research content silently dropped. transient error sequential research also caught skipped rather aborting whole run. tempest_config(search_provider = ) searches now apply request timeout retry transient HTTP errors, single missing non-public result URL longer discards every result query. Co-STORM sessions now route turns, update mind map, summarise using recent dialogue turns instead oldest. Persisted runs now write artifacts atomically write run manifest last, interrupted save corrupt artifacts leave resume pointing stage whose output missing. bundled Shiny app now targets shinychat’s development chat_server() API streaming, cancellation, greetings, client state management. bundled Shiny app rebuilt around Shiny modules (one per tab) shared reactive store, runs STORM pipeline background ExtendedTask bound task button, adds knowledge-stats value-box strip Mind Map tab, landing welcome message, “” popover linking STORM, Co-STORM, DSPy papers upstream repositories.
